title: Value-Momentum Combo
thesis: Combining value and momentum factors can enhance returns by capturing both mean reversion and trend-following effects. The model selects stocks that rank highly on value metrics but also exhibit positive recent price momentum, aiming to exploit underpriced assets with upward price trends. This dual-factor approach seeks to balance the strengths of each signal and reduce exposure to their individual weaknesses.
signal_logic: 1) Calculate the trailing 12-month price return excluding the most recent month (11 months total) for momentum. 2) Calculate the Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio as a proxy for value using most recent annual earnings and current price. 3) Rank universe stocks by momentum and value separately. 4) Select stocks in the top 30% by momentum and bottom 30% by P/E (lowest P/E).
entry_exit: Entry: Buy selected stocks at monthly rebalance if they meet both momentum and value criteria. Exit: Sell stocks that fall outside the combined top 30% momentum and bottom 30% P/E ranks at monthly rebalance.
position_sizing: Equal weight positions across all selected stocks each month, with maximum allocation per stock capped at 5% to avoid concentration risk.
risks: Value metrics like P/E can be distorted during earnings anomalies or sector-specific cycles. Momentum can lead to overexposure in bubbles or sudden reversals. Combining factors may reduce but not eliminate drawdowns. Small universe may limit diversification.
implementation_notes: 1) Download monthly adjusted close prices and earnings data from yfinance. 2) Calculate trailing returns excluding last month for momentum. 3) Extract latest earnings and calculate P/E ratios. 4) Rank stocks and select per criteria. 5) Rebalance portfolio monthly. 6) Track portfolio returns and turnover.
